exemplar.title: How Successful Has Cutting Corporation Tax Been in Stimulating Foreign Direct Investment into the Greek Economy Since 2021?

detailedExemplarData.title: How Successful Has Cutting Corporation Tax Been in Stimulating Foreign Direct Investment into the Greek Economy Since 2021?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Explicit, wellâfocused research question and clear link to data selection and analysis
Consistent focus on the 2021 tax cutâs effect on Greek FDI throughout
Appropriate range of secondary sources with justification of selection
weaknesses: Minor digressions (e.g., macro-benefits section) slightly dilute tight focus
Methodology discussion could include more detail on criteria for source reliability
Opportunity to deepen justification of source selection
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student meets many descriptors of the top bandâclear topic communication, a sharply defined question and informed source selectionâthere are small lapses in methodological detail and occasional digressions. This places the work solidly in the mid-band for Focus and Method, warranting 3 out of 6.

strengths: Strong disciplinary knowledge with clear explanations of tax competition and FDI concepts
Accurate and consistent use of economic terminology and correctly labelled diagrams
Relevant integration of comparative data (Italy) and contextual background on Greece
weaknesses: Some source citations are listed without deeper analysis
Occasional reliance on generic textbook references rather than primary literature
A few data presentations lack unit consistency (e.g., billions vs millions)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates coherent subject knowledge and uses terminology effectively, but the application of source material occasionally lacks depth and consistency. These characteristics align with the middle band of Knowledge and Understanding, justifying 3 out of 6.

strengths: Majority of research sources are appropriate and clearly linked to the research question
Analysis is logical and supported by evidence (pre/post averages, Italy benchmark)
Arguments include a reasoned evaluation of limitations and alternative determinants
weaknesses: Quantitative treatment remains basic (simple RÂ² without full regression details)
Some critical evaluations accept claims uncritically (e.g., coastline effect)
Opportunities to strengthen robustness (confidence intervals, statistical criteria) are not fully realized
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently gathers relevant research and offers clear, mostly well-supported analysis. Critical evaluation is present but not at the highest level of methodological sophistication. Overall this merits a solid upper-mid band performance in Critical Thinking, scored as 6 out of 12.

strengths: Adheres to IB structure conventions with clear headings, contents and logical flow
Consistent layout, readable tables and correctly applied formatting for title and page numbers
Comprehensive table of contents enhances navigation
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation style (mix of ibid and full references)
References are not fully alphabetized and formatting varies
Footnote placement for tables could be more reader-friendly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally strong, following required conventions with only minor issues in citation and reference formatting. These small inconsistencies place it in the mid-band for Presentation, warranting 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly worded (âHow successful has cutting corporation tax beenâ¦â) and is narrow in time-frame (since 2021) and place (Greece), providing an effective focus that is directly revisited in the data, analysis and conclusion sections.  Because this guiding question structures the whole argument and links logically to the chosen data sets, the clarity and focus match the 5-6 band.

The student states an explicit title and research purpose in the opening section, and throughout the essay the focus on the 2021 corporation-tax cut and its effect on FDI in Greece is maintained.  Headings, data tables and the comparison with Italy all reinforce a clearly delineated area of investigation that is well-suited to systematic economic analysis.  Minor digressions (e.g., lengthy macro-benefits section) do not undermine the overall clarity, so the descriptor for a clear and appropriate purpose is met at the upper band level.

Most data employed (Bank of Greece tables, R-squared calculation, Italy comparator) are relevant to the question and integrated into the narrative.  However, some citations are merely listed rather than analysed, and a few broad assertions rely on generic textbook references.  Overall application is mostly appropriate but not consistently strong, aligning with the middle band.

The essay follows IB EE conventions: title page, abstract substitute, contents page, introduction, analysis, conclusion, references.  Headings and sub-headings give a logical flow; no required element appears missing, so structure is clearly appropriate (upper band).

Word count, title and page references are provided; in-text citations and tables are formatted consistently, and visuals are readable.  Minor spacing and table glitches are present but do not impede readability.  Hence layout considerations are present and correctly applied, warranting the top presentation band.

Arguments are reasoned and the conclusion synthesises findings and caveats; the student evaluates alternative determinants and methodological limits.  However, the evaluation remains largely qualitative, and some claims (e.g., 158-168% coastline effect) are accepted uncritically.  This delivers partial critical evaluationâappropriate for the middle of the 4-6 band.

The student offers quantitative comparison (pre/post-2021 averages, Italy benchmark) and theoretical discussion (tax competition, infrastructure theory).  Analysis is logical and usually backed by evidence, but causality is acknowledged as weak and statistical treatment is limited to a simple RÂ².  Thus analysis is clear and mostly supported, fitting the 4-6 band.

The essay demonstrates solid disciplinary knowledge: concepts such as tax competition, trade-openness, multiplier and LRAS are explained correctly and linked to FDI theory.  Economic context on Greece and comparative data with Italy show coherent understanding, supported by academic literature, warranting placement in the top band.

Research is largely appropriate (national data series, OECD, Transparency International) and clearly tied to the question, but the time-series is short and some projected figures weaken robustness.  Nonetheless, the majority of material is relevant and suitable, meriting a secure 4-6 band score.

An extensive list of secondary sources (national bank data, OECD, academic papers) is marshalled, and the student explains criteria and limitations (projection data, lack of primary access).  Although only secondary research is undertaken, the range is broad and clearly relevant; justification for source selection and acknowledgement of shortcomings shows informed choice, satisfying âappropriate range â¦ effective and informed selection.â

Terminology (FDI types, CPI, shadow economy, RÂ², marginal tax rate) is used accurately and consistently.  Diagrams are correctly labelled, and economic abbreviations are standard.  The precision and consistency meet the highest descriptor for this strand.

annotations.comment: The title is clearly stated and precisely reflects the investigationâs focus on corporate tax cuts and FDI in Greece, enhancing reader orientation.
The research question is succinct and wellâfocused on the 2021 tax cutâs impact on Greek FDI, providing clear guidance for data selection and analysis.
Providing the word count at the start meets EE formatting requirements, demonstrating attention to IB administrative conventions.
The table of contents is comprehensive and logically ordered, enhancing navigation and reflecting a clear structural plan.
The introduction defines FDI effectively, but could more directly link this definition to Greeceâs specific economic context to strengthen relevance.
Contextual background on Greeceâs postâ2008 crisis is well presented, demonstrating solid knowledge of the economic environment.
Citation style in footnotes appears inconsistent (mix of ibid and full refs); standardize formatting for clarity and academic rigor.
The hypothesis is clearly stated and grounded in global trends of tax competition, aligning theory and research focus effectively.
Methodology discussion shows informed source selection but would benefit from more detail on criteria used to judge source reliability.
Acknowledging reliance on secondary data and its limitations shows critical awareness of methodological constraints.
Justifying the inclusion of nonâEuropean studies (Laudage, Abdullaev) could clarify applicability to Greeceâs context.
The project acknowledges Bank of Greece projections as uncertain; this critical evaluation enhances research credibility.
The data table is clear and appropriately labeled but would gain from footnote placement directly below it to avoid reader confusion.
Table 1 effectively presents preâ and postâcut FDI, supporting the investigationâs quantitative basis.
References are not fully alphabetized and formatting varies; standardize to a consistent style (APA/MLA) to enhance professionalism.
The reference list on page 25 demonstrates minor inconsistencies in italics and punctuation; a final proofread would ensure uniformity.
The RÂ² explanation smartly distinguishes correlation from causation; consider reporting the regression equation for fuller transparency.
Excluding 2020 as an outlier is reasonable, but detailing statistical criteria for outlier removal would strengthen the analysis.
Comparison with Italy is a valuable benchmark, but clarify unit consistency (billions vs millions) to avoid misinterpretation.
Normalizing FDI by GDP early in the analysis could improve crossâcountry comparability in the Italy comparison.
Incorporating the Tax Competition theory demonstrates strong use of terminology and links concept to Greek policy context.
Corruption index discussion is pertinent but would benefit from annual trend data to contextualize 2023âs value.
The shadow economy analysis adds nuance; consider including confidence intervals or sensitivity checks for robustness.
Government revenue data effectively challenges expected taxâcut trade-off; consider extending analysis with multiâyear trend comparisons.
The trade openness discussion is clear but relies on 2018 EU averages; updating to more recent figures would improve relevance.
Applying Abdullaevâs CISâbased coastline effect to Greece needs caution; contexts differ significantly.
Consider sourcing an infrastructure index (e.g., World Bank) to quantitatively assess endowment rather than rely on qualitative argument.
The industry breakdown table is informative; adding percentage change comparisons would deepen analysis of sectoral shifts.
Linking specific infrastructure needs for top FDI sectors displays coherent application of economic concepts.
Discussion of the E65 motorway and RRP demonstrates realâworld application but should include project timelines to anchor analysis.
Reflection on nonâcompletion of most infrastructure projects shows evaluative depth regarding causality limitations.
Conclusion reiterates key findings effectively but would benefit from quantitative summary of effect sizes.
Methodological limitations section critically addresses generalizability but could include reflection on dataâprojection uncertainty.
